content.claim: Children starting school one year later gain stronger performance benefits than those starting on-time, with effects much stronger for students from low-educated families, attributed to better skill-development in kindergarten versus schools for low-status children.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: This finding concerns delayed school entry rather than gimnázium tracking directly
The mechanism (kindergarten quality advantage) is distinct from academic selection via competitive exam
Results may not generalize from age-based selection to track-based (academic) selection, as researchers caution against uniform effects across different selection mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
